title: Use unpredictable initialization vectors
description: Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode requires unpredictable initialization vectors (IVs) to ensure security. Predictable or reused IVs can allow attackers to deduce information about encrypted data or perform cryptographic attacks.
recommendation: Generate cryptographically secure random IVs for each encryption operation using CBC mode.

noncompliant.description: Using a fixed or predictable initialization vector makes the encryption vulnerable to attacks.
Reusing the same IV across multiple encryptions compromises security.

compliant.description: Generate a cryptographically secure random IV for each encryption operation.
